title: Snogar

{{Quote|Snogars not hurt smart ones! We need you and other off-worlders! You come!|A Snogar, to [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]|<ref name="ota" />}}
'''Snogars''' were a tall, furry [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the [[Water/Legends|ice]]-covered [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ota]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. The Snogars were descended from a technologically advanced race referred to as the "Old Ones," who built [[City/Legends|cities]] within Ota's ice canyons. Over time the Snogars forgot how to use their ancestors' technology, and reverted to primitive lifestyles inside their ancient cities. By [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], that had resulted in a major crisis, as the Snogars were unable to power the heating machines that kept their homes habitable, threatening their way of life on their frigid [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Snogars were a furry,<ref name="egas" /> [[Ape/Legends|apelike]]<ref name="egtc">''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] from the frozen<ref name="egas" /> [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]<ref name="atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Ota]]. Physically imposing—adult Snogars averaged 2.5 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall—and of limited intelligence, Snogars struggled to survive in Ota's cold climate despite being native to the world, relying on heating machines in their [[City/Legends|cities]] to live with any comfort.<ref name="egas" /> Snogars had large manes of fur surrounding their heads, with simian, purple faces that they often kept hidden with masks meant to protect against Ota's cold.<ref name="ota" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|Old Ones make city... make power... many cycles past! But... Old Ones all die!|A Snogar, to Luke Skywalker|<ref name="ota" />}}
[[File:SnogarsTogetherStrong-CSWTEA9.jpg|thumb|left|250px|An angry mob of Snogars attack Boba Fett.]]
The Snogars were descended from a highly intelligent civilization later remembered as the "Old Ones," who built cities within the [[Water/Legends|ice]] canyons of Ota. However, they eventually forgot how to use their advanced technology and became the Snogars, who carved out primitive existence in the ruins of their forefathers. The Snogars still managed to effectively use some simple technologies, such as [[stun net]]s, and traversed the planet's snow drifts on snowboard-like conveyors called [[powerslider]]s.<ref name="ota" />

==History==
{{Quote|Snogars need you! You off-worlder... you smart! You come, fix our ice-power!|A Snogar, to Luke Skywalker|<ref name="ota" />}}
Civilization flourished on Ota in the millennia prior to the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], but over the course of thousands of years, the intelligent and technologically savvy "Old Ones" devolved into the primitive Snogars, who struggled to maintain the devices that kept their society habitable.<ref name="egas" /> By [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], the situation had grown desperate, as the Snogars were no longer able to operate the [[Hydroelectric power generator|hydroelectric generators]] that powered the heating machines that were vital to their survival. The Snogars thus began searching for offworlders who perhaps had the technical knowledge to aid them. Their prayers were seemingly answered when a group of Snogars captured [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] and attempted to force them to fix their generators, but the encounter only resulted in a few dead Snogars and the natives fleeing their city from a vengeful Fett.<ref name="ota" />

==Behind the scenes==
Snogars first appeared in ''[[The Frozen World of Ota]]'', a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] arc written by [[Russ Manning]], [[Rick Hoberg]] and [[Don Christensen]] and released in [[1980]].<ref name="ota" />
